name: Azure Container Instances
what_it_does: Runs Docker containers on-demand in Azure without managing VMs or orchestrators — billed per second of CPU and memory consumption.
use_cases: Running ephemeral agent task workers that spin up on demand and terminate after job completion
Executing batch ML inference jobs in isolated containers without provisioning a Kubernetes cluster
Hosting short-lived API backends or webhook processors that scale to zero between bursts
Running containerized data pipeline steps as discrete, isolated compute units
Executing test suites or CI jobs in clean container environments via Azure DevOps integration
not_for: Long-running stateful services needing persistent orchestration, health monitoring, and rolling deploys — use AKS instead
Workloads requiring GPU acceleration at scale — ACI GPU SKUs are limited and costly vs dedicated GPU VMs
Multi-container microservice meshes with service discovery — ACI container groups are limited to co-located containers
best_when: You need fast, isolated container execution for burst or batch workloads and want zero cluster management overhead.
avoid_when: You need sustained high-throughput container workloads where Kubernetes-based autoscaling would be more cost-effective.

auth.notes: Azure AD / Entra ID service principals or managed identities are the recommended auth path; access can be scoped to specific resource groups via RBAC roles.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 180,000 vCPU-seconds and 180,000 GB-seconds free per month
pricing.paid_tiers: Linux containers: $0.0000125/vCPU-second, $0.0000013/GB-second; Windows containers approximately 4x higher; spot pricing available at ~60% discount
pricing.notes: Billed from container start to stop in 1-second increments; network egress charged separately; persistent volume mounts (Azure Files) charged at storage rates.

performance.rate_limits: Default subscription limit of 100 container groups per region; CPU/memory limits per container group (4 vCPU / 16 GB for Linux); limits raiseable via support request

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ARM API returns structured JSON errors with code and message fields; provisioning failures surface in container group state rather than HTTP error, requiring polling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: PUT operations on container groups are idempotent by resource name within a resource group; DELETE is safe to retry; container restarts are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Cold start latency is 5-30 seconds for container image pull — cache images in Azure Container Registry in the same region to reduce this
Container groups cannot be updated in place for most properties — you must delete and recreate, causing downtime
Log streaming via the REST API has a 4MB limit per call; long-running container stdout requires Azure Log Analytics integration
Spot/evictable containers can be terminated with no warning — agents must checkpoint state externally or use restart policies
ARM API throttling applies at subscription level across all Azure services — heavy ACI automation can exhaust shared ARM quota

security_score_components.security_notes: Entra ID RBAC provides fine-grained access control; managed identity support eliminates credential management; containers can mount Key Vault secrets; network isolation via VNet injection supported.
